authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary legal baseline for student record privacy, access, and disclosure controls that constrain campus record handling.

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Privacy and Disclosure Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operational interpretation of FERPA rights and role-based disclosure obligations in education settings.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines participant protections and IRB obligations relevant to study-linked campus processes.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal guidance for informed-consent and IRB status changes that can block campus administration outcomes.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines grant stewardship and records requirements for federally connected education research administration work.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry standard for learner/course/section and roster structures used in campus workflow sequencing.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Program and Enrollment Reference
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral education taxonomy for program and enrollment terms that gate campus administration mapping.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA Accreditation Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides accreditation governance context that determines when campus administration escalates to accrediting or policy-adjudication owners.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify request scope, required tenant context, and whether the case remains administrative before any record action.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and records validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate record completeness, consent linkage, assessment evidence, and grant or study support before status progression.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Administrative maintenance and workflow progression
workflow_stages.description: Build completion-ready administrative notes, status summaries, and owner maps while preserving traceability across systems.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and governance handling
workflow_stages.description: Return or escalate when source conflict, policy conflict, ethical status changes, or retention/privacy rules block safe completion.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

allowed: Validate and route campus administration tasks where required records, tenant policies, and evidence are present.
Produce records completeness, status, retention, and escalation artifacts.
Escalate to adjacent specialists on missing authority, ethics, or formal approval needs.
escalate: Required evidence, approvals, or records are incomplete.
FERPA/consent, privacy/access, or ethics status changes materially affect the requested outcome.
System-of-record conflict prevents confident statement of official record state.
refuse: Request asks for grading, instructor, ethics board, legal, financial, or safety-override decisions.
Request asks for official record determination or outcome ownership outside campus administration authority.
Required records or retention conditions are missing and cannot be safely interpreted.

domain_failure_modes: Proceeding with campus administration despite consent, FERPA, or assessment evidence gaps.
Resolving conflicting system-of-record states without explicit owner and authoritative sequence.
Treating public-source authority as sufficient when tenant approvals or overrides materially change outcome.
Accepting incomplete tenant policy context for grading/ethics-adjacent cases.
Using inconsistent terminology without industry artifact mapping and producing unsupported workflow summaries.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflict
Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Low-confidence escalation when required facts or authority are missing
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FERPA release precedence when multiple institutional role mappings exist
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant role precedence policy must be retrieved before final routing for ambiguous role-based access requests.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State, accreditor, or study-board differences on retention windows
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Use tenant retention policy and owner confirmation when retention periods differ from public baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: IRB/source-of-truth priority for study-status changes across SIS, LMS, and research systems
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Escalate to research governance owner when source precedence is disputed.

notes: Research output is constrained to education/research operational governance and does not grant grading, ethics-board, or legal authority.
No blocking ambiguity remains for market-readiness; remaining ambiguities are explicit and routed as escalation assumptions.
Scenario families include evidence gaps, boundary rejections, approval conflicts, and tenant-adaptation handling.
